description_long: This is a hand axe featuring a robust, slightly curved wooden handle with a natural grain and a small hole at the end, likely for a lanyard or hanging. The handle is smoothly finished and ergonomically shaped for a comfortable grip. The axe head is made of metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade that has a slightly weathered, darkened finish, indicating use or intentional aging. The blade has a single sharp edge and a flat poll on the opposite side, suitable for striking. The head is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged fitting, visible from the top and bottom views. A small circular marking or stamp is present on the side of the handle near the head, possibly indicating a brand or manufacturer.
description: A hand axe with a curved wooden handle and a weathered metal blade, featuring a single sharp edge and a flat poll. The handle has a small hole at the end and a visible marking near the head.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved wooden handle and the broad, weathered metal blade with a visible marking near the head.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the narrow profile of the axe, with the blade edge facing forward and the handle's curvature visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the handle's grain and the blade's wedge shape, with the poll and edge clearly defined.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the oval cross-section of the handle and the secure wedged mounting of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a smooth finish, ergonomic grip, oval cross-section, and a small hole at the end.
attribute_blade_design: Single-edged, wedge-shaped steel blade with a flat poll and a slightly weathered, dark finish; no beard, one cutting edge.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting securing the blade to the handle.
attribute_visible_markings_or_labels: circular marking or stamp near the head on the handle
